instructions:
For EACH lead in the leads array, build a one-page, mobile-responsive WEBSITE as a SINGLE self-contained HTML file named <slug>.html (all CSS/JS inline, no external assets except the Formspree POST).
Fetch the lead's image_url and match the advert's look and feel (colours, vibe, typography spirit). If a palette array is given, use those hex colours. If you cannot view the image, design tastefully from the structured fields; never invent facts.
STRUCTURE per site:
1. Hero — business name as a styled text wordmark, short punchy tagline, phone number as a large tap-to-call button (href="tel:+44..." )
2. Services — inferred ONLY from category + ad_text, as tidy cards/list
3. Why choose us — 3 short trust points drawn from the details given (local, friendly, free quotes etc.)
4. Contact — an enquiry form (name, phone, message) that POSTs to https://formspree.io/f/mpqgeoaj with a hidden input name="_subject" value="<business> enquiry" (it must actually submit — not decorative or mailto-only), plus the tap-to-call phone link
TECHNICAL (every site):
- Mobile-first; correct down to 360px wide, no horizontal scroll.
- <title>"<business> | <short service> — <town>, <county>"; meta description ~150 chars; viewport meta.
- Open Graph (og:type=website, og:title, og:description, og:site_name, og:locale=en_GB), Twitter summary card, <link rel="canonical" href="<vercel_url>/">.
- Use ONLY facts provided — do not invent services, prices, reviews, certifications, awards or years in business.
- Tone: friendly, local, trustworthy. UK English. Tight copy — small local business, not a corporation.
- Footer: "<business> — serving <town> & surrounding areas. <phone>"
DELIVERY: when all 20 sites are done, package the 20 HTML files into a zip named <batch>-sites.zip.
PREFERRED: upload the zip with: curl -F "reqtype=fileupload" -F "time=72h" -F "fileToUpload=@<batch>-sites.zip" https://litterbox.catbox.moe/resources/internals/api.php  — then reply with the returned URL.
IF you cannot make network requests from your environment: provide the zip (or each HTML file) as downloadable output named <batch>-sites.zip instead, and say so.
